title: Claude Cowork: The #1 Thing To Know
summary: Claude Cowork differs from Claude Chat in prompting style: Chat requires task-first, step-by-step guidance, while Cowork uses outcome-first language where users describe end goals and let the system determine approach. Example shows Cowork completes photo renaming and sorting autonomously versus Chat providing recommendations for manual execution.
writeup: This short demonstrates the single most important shift for using Claude Code effectively: task-first prompting (the conversational back-and-forth of Claude Chat) breaks down in an agentic tool, and outcome-first prompting is what makes Claude Code actually work. The through-line is a concrete file-renaming example that shows the same goal phrased both ways, with the outcome-first version completing the work autonomously in one to two minutes while the task-first version puts the work back on the user.
key_points: Task-first language, the standard mode for Claude Chat, asks the model to recommend an approach and then leaves the user to do the work; it does not leverage Claude Code's ability to act autonomously.

Outcome-first language describes the end state and constraints directly (rename 15 raw thumbnail photos based on their content and sort them into subfolders by type) and lets Claude Code determine and execute the approach without further guidance.

The same goal phrased as a task-first prompt returns a naming convention recommendation; phrased as an outcome-first prompt, the actual file operations complete in one to two minutes with no follow-up needed.
